10x Launches - Office Hours 1
Bonus Themeplate: “The Piglet”
The Template:
[Declare Your Anxiety]
[Add Personal Details to Build Intimacy]
[Why You’re Concerned]
[Reason 1: Crunchy MOHT which they’ll continue to experience]
[Reason 2: Emotional MOHT which they’ll continue to experience]
[Reason 3: Contrasting the two possible outcomes]
[Reason 4: It denies you of your big WHY or company’s mission]
[Stack the most logical, inarguable, no-brainer reasons to buy]
[Confident final pitch of why your prospect needs what you have]
[PS: Close with Proof in order to balance out all the feels]
Example: Copy School (March 2017)
My BFF Ange hates it when I worry.
My cats Lily and Puff hate it when I stress.
The cashier guy at Tim Horton's hates it when I get that "furrowed" look right between my eyebrows - he
knows even a twenty-pack of Timbits won't fix it.
And obviously...
I don't like worrying.
Yet here's why you've got my stomach all tied up in knots.
After today, I won't be able to work with you inside Copy School
Which means...
... you'll still struggle the next time you're supposed to produce a high-converting email campaign and the
landing pages it drives to. When I could've helped.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 5 --

... you'll still think of copywriting as something you guess at, and as a consequence you'll still actually guess
at it, and as a consequence you'll believe you're just guessing wrong and you'll try one more time, and as a
consequence you'll give up. When I could've helped.
... you'll still live in a world where you never, ever get that feeling that comes with publishing new copy...
and stepping away from the screen with a cheeky grin that says, "Damn right - I wrote that." When I
could've helped.
And in short, I won't be able to make good on all the reasons Copy Hackers exists in the first place.
So ya.
Bit of an issue.
Because Copy School is the closest thing to a shortcut to high-converting copy that exists. There is no
bot for this. And the copywriters you'd love to hire to do this work are really, really hard to find... really,
really expensive when you find them... and nearly impossible to get on the calendars of.
And even then --- you won't know if their work hits the mark until after you've paid a hefty deposit and
waited weeks to get their proposed copy.
And even then --- you won't have more than your gut to go on when it's time to review their copy. Is it
persuasive? Who knows. Does it follow the rules? You don't know the rules. Are there better practices at
play - or did this copywriter learn everything about copywriting from a few blog posts, which were
themselves based on other blog posts...? Did your freelance copywriter get their training at the end of a
game of telephone?
So I'm worried.
I'm worried that you'll miss out on this in-your-grasp opportunity to take that "next step" that's eluded you.
I can't think of ANY other skillset where the gap between those who do it well and those who don't is so
wide.
But the best part is...
I also can't think of any other skill where that gap could be bridged faster and with more predictable results.
I created Copy School to be that bridge.
Join today - take your risk-free shot and start crossing that bridge with me
Today's the last day to get in (and to view our workshops), and we close enrolment in:
~jo :)
PS. Need a glimpse of what it's like to cross that bridge?
Here's what Marian Schembari experienced after taking 10x Emails, which is just one of 3 programs you'll
get immediate access to inside Copy School:
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 5 --

"Before 10X I knew how to write a good email. But I didn't know to write a 10x email. And holy crap did
this course deliver on its promise. I did indeed 10x.... I 10xed my client's investment (I helped
Teachable make $500k using this sequence). I 10xed my hourly rate. I 10xed my available daylight
hours. (Seriously, I now charge $500 per email and using these email templates it now only takes me
~1 hour to write.)"
Execute on all your growth plans by conquering copywriting inside Copy School
